code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test03-MAR-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, mild ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), with low specific gravity. No evidence of urinary tract infection (negative nitrites, leukocytes). Findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or another glomerular pathology; further nephrological evaluation and assessment of glycemic control are recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
